fields.architectural_role: encoder-only (multi-layer bidirectional Transformer encoder; no decoder)
fields.stack_depth: BERT_BASE: L=12, H=768, A=12, 110M parameters; BERT_LARGE: L=24, H=1024, A=16, 340M parameters

markdown:
## Architectural Role: Encoder-only

BERT uses the Transformer **encoder only** — it does **not** use a Transformer decoder. Section 3 of the BERT paper states verbatim:

> "BERT's model architecture is a **multi-layer bidirectional Transformer encoder** based on the original implementation described in Vaswani et al. (2017) and released in the tensor2tensor library."

This is reinforced by BERT's own name expansion (Abstract / Introduction): **B**idirectional **E**ncoder **R**epresentations from **T**ransformers. No decoder stack is described or instantiated anywhere in the architecture section.

## Supporting Details

| Aspect | Value | Evidence |
|---|---|---|
| Architectural role | Multi-layer bidirectional Transformer **encoder** (Vaswani et al., 2017); no decoder | §3, Model Architecture |
| Bidirectionality mechanism | Masked Language Model (MLM) pre-training objective (Cloze-style) | §1, Introduction |
| Self-attention masking | Full / bidirectional self-attention (no causal mask), in contrast to GPT's left-only constrained self-attention | §3 |
| Stack depth | BERT_BASE: L=12, H=768, A=12, 110M params; BERT_LARGE: L=24, H=1024, A=16, 340M params | §3 |
| Decoder used? | **No** | §3 (none referenced) |

## Direct Quoted Evidence

1. "BERT's model architecture is a **multi-layer bidirectional Transformer encoder** based on the original implementation described in Vaswani et al. (2017)…" — §3.
2. "the **BERT Transformer uses bidirectional self-attention**, while the GPT Transformer uses constrained self-attention where every token can only attend to context to its left." — §3.
3. "the MLM objective enables the representation to fuse the left and the right context, which allows us to **pretrain a deep bidirectional Transformer**." — §1.
summary: BERT uses the Transformer as an encoder only (multi-layer bidirectional Transformer encoder), with no decoder, based on the original Vaswani et al. (2017) implementation.
